reviews.review_description: Nice game does exactly what it says. Can’t give it five stars though because I literally finish the game in 10 minutes but it’s free so you can’t really criticize a free thing.￼

reviews.review_title: Nice idea, but needs more polish
reviews.review_description:
I like the concept, and relaxed feel. Graphics are adequate, animations are a little janky. The main problem is the gameplay requires a bit too much precision. It should allow you to pick up all the pieces in the same way, but some of them act differently to others. Some snap to akward angles when you pick them up. Others can be reoriented by placing them then picking them up at a different angle. Some let you place them at any orientation, others have to be precisely the right way around.

All of the mechanisms are animated, some with flaws. The last puzzle is stuttery on my Quest 3. The one with the lifting squares has lift-slots that the pegs don't fit correctly. One of them has a square peg that's misaligned to the handle that fits on it.

All minor flaws I suppose, but thats literally the entire app. Interesting mechanisms, but kind of limited gameplay.

Handling pieces is clunky and sometimes difficult to grasp them and orient. Main issue for me is that on my last mechanism to complete, one of the gear pieces will not lock in place so unable to complete the mechanism. Have restarted the mechanism twice and see the same issue. Have submitted a bug report to the developer.
Nice and well made game. I like it, but way too short - only 20 - 30 min...
I really enjoyed building mechanisms and I felt like an inventor. I want new tools to combine them!
